libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not add lucene-analyzers-common or other Lucene modules just to reach lucene-core coverage. Keep coverage focused on classes present in lucene-core. For custom AttributeImpl subclasses, Lucene 6 makes reflectWith(AttributeReflector) abstract, so tests must implement it explicitly. If tests exercise MMapDirectory/RamUsageEstimator-style internals, keep the test JVM/native execution opens used by the existing Lucene tests: --add-opens=java.base/java.nio=ALL-UNNAMED and --add-opens=java.base/jdk.internal.ref=ALL-UNNAMED.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Lucene examples often use StandardAnalyzer from lucene-analyzers-common, but that is outside lucene-core and should not be added unless a specific test intentionally covers cross-module integration.
Native Image/JDK encapsulation behavior around MMapDirectory unmapping and RamUsageEstimator remains sensitive to JDK version; local Gradle and Native Image verification are authoritative.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: lucene-core 6.0.0 is a self-contained core artifact with no Maven runtime dependencies and no external service requirement; the needed preparation is test-code/JVM guidance for Lucene 6 API changes and JDK-internal access used by existing meaningful coverage.
